page: UpCloud GPU-8xCPU-64GB-1xL40S is a KVM-based virtual server featuring an AMD EPYC 9575F processor with 8 shared vCPUs and 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Graphics and compute acceleration are handled by a single NVIDIA L40S GPU from the Ada Lovelace architecture, equipped with 48 GB of dedicated VRAM. This instance does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be handled externally.

In benchmark evaluations, the server exhibits top-tier single-core CPU performance, with both the stress-ng single-core test and Geekbench single-core score ranking in the top 10%. Single-threaded data processing is highly efficient, as demonstrated by top-tier results in gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. Conversely, multi-core CPU performance, including Geekbench multi-core and PassMark CPU scores, falls within the average range.

The integrated NVIDIA L40S GPU delivers strong performance for specialized machine learning workloads. Large Language Model inference benchmarks show top-tier text generation speeds of 448.64 tokens per second for small 135M models, and strong text generation speeds of 33.46 tokens per second for medium 7B models. However, prompt processing for large 70B models represents a performance bottleneck, registering in the bottom 25% at 6.59 tokens per second.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, this server offers a balanced resource density for GPU-accelerated tasks that do not require dedicated local storage. The combination of top-tier single-core CPU performance and 48 GB of GPU VRAM makes this instance highly suitable for medium-scale machine learning inference, image processing, text processing, and general GPU-accelerated compute workloads.
description: UpCloud GPU-8xCPU-64GB-1xL40S is a KVM-based virtual server designed for GPU-accelerated workloads. It features an AMD EPYC 9575F processor with 8 shared vCPUs, 64.0 GB of system memory, and a single NVIDIA L40S GPU with 48 GB of VRAM. The instance does not include local storage. In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong memory performance, though multi-core CPU metrics remain average. The NVIDIA L40S GPU delivers strong text generation speeds for small and medium-sized language models, though prompt processing performance drops significantly for larger 70B models. This hardware profile is highly suitable for medium-scale machine learning inference, image processing, and single-threaded data processing tasks that require substantial GPU memory without local storage dependencies.
og_description: UpCloud GPU-8xCPU-64GB-1xL40S is a KVM virtual server featuring 8 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA L40S GPU with 48 GB VRAM, optimized for medium-scale AI inference and image processing.
meta_description: UpCloud GPU-8xCPU-64GB-1xL40S is a KVM server with 8 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 48 GB NVIDIA L40S GPU, designed for GPU-accelerated workloads.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated virtual server featuring an NVIDIA L40S GPU and AMD EPYC processor for machine learning and data processing workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 9575F processor with 8 shared vCPUs and 64 GB RAM
NVIDIA L40S GPU with 48 GB VRAM for accelerated computing
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Strong text generation speeds for small and medium LLM inference
No local storage included, requiring external storage solutions
Best suited for medium-scale AI inference and image processing
